subject: Conditional SMTP Authencation
body: Hello, We want to require SMTP AUTH for users outside for "all" e-mails. The important point is "all" should include emails sent to the mydomain.com. In our configuration - e-mails which sent by mydomain.com users to domains except for mydomain.com requires SMTP AUTH. - e-mails which has recipient as mydomain.com is accepted because of permit_auth_destinations. What We want is to - e-mails sent by mydomain.com users (inside or outside of the network) to mydomain.com should require SMTP AUTH. - e-mails sent by any other domains to mydomain.com is accepted as should. Our configuration is listed below: smtpd_sender_restrictions = permit_mynetworks, permit_sasl_authenticated, reject_invalid_hostname, reject_unknown_sender_domain, reject_non_fqdn_sender, permit smtpd_recipient_restrictions = permit_mynetworks, permit_sasl_authenticated, reject_unauth_pipelining, permit_auth_destination, reject_unknown_recipient_domain, reject_non_fqdn_recipient, reject_unauth_destination, reject_rbl_client zen.spamhaus.org, reject_rbl_client list.dsbl.org, reject_rbl_client multihop.dsbl.org, reject_rbl_client dnsbl.njabl.org, Regards, Oguz Yilmaz

subject: Security Protocols for Medical Data on Ubuntu
body: I am inquiring about the recommended security measures for storing medical data on Ubuntu systems. Given the high sensitivity of medical data, it is imperative to safeguard its confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Could you provide information on the best practices for encrypting medical data, securing access controls, and defending against unauthorized access? Moreover, I would be grateful for any advice on adhering to regulatory requirements like HIPAA. I look forward to your response and further discussion on securing medical data on Ubuntu systems.

subject: Wir warten auf Ihre Rückmeldung - We are waiting for your feedback
body: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren wir sind ein erfahrener Hersteller von E Bikes mit eigenem Lager in Deutschland und bieten derzeit eine attraktive Sonderaktion an Dank unseres deutschen Lagers können wir Bestellungen innerhalb von 3 bis 5 Tagen europaweit ausliefern Aktuell haben wir ein leistungsstarkes faltbares 20 Zoll Fat Tire E Bike im Angebot Motor 500W für starke Leistung und eine maximale Geschwindigkeit von 45 kmh Akku 48V 15Ah Lithium Batterie hohe Reichweite und herausnehmbar Design Kompakt und faltbar ideal für Stadt und Outdoor Abenteuer Falls Sie Interesse haben lassen Sie uns einfach Ihre Lieferadresse zukommen und wir erstellen Ihnen ein individuelles Angebot die Preise können je nach Zielort leicht variieren Zusätzlich suchen wir engagierte Händler und Vertriebspartner in Europa die gemeinsam mit uns wachsen möchten Falls Sie Interesse an einer Kooperation haben freuen wir uns auf Ihre Nachricht Für ein konkretes Angebot oder eine Bestellung senden Sie uns bitte die gewünschte Lieferadresse zu damit wir die genauen Versandkosten berechnen können Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Rückmeldung Mit freundlichen Grüßen Johannes Keller Hi, We are a professional manufacturer of E Bikes with our own warehouse in Germany and are pleased to offer high quality electric bicycles with fast and reliable delivery Thanks to our warehouse in Germany we can ship orders across Europe within 3 to 5 days ensuring a smooth and efficient purchasing experience for our customers Currently we have a special promotion on one of our most popular models a 20 inch Fat Tire Foldable E Bike This model is designed for both urban commuting and off road adventures making it an excellent choice for riders looking for versatility and convenience Key features of this E Bike Motor 500W high performance motor allowing a maximum speed of 45 kmh ensuring a powerful and smooth riding experience Battery 48V 15Ah lithium battery providing a long range and easy removability for convenient charging Design Compact and foldable making it easy to store and transport perfect for daily commuters and outdoor enthusiasts Versatility Suitable for a wide range of terrains including city streets mountain trails beaches and even snowy roads Comfort Equipped with front suspension and a sturdy frame for enhanced stability and shock absorption In addition to selling directly to customers we are actively looking for dealers and business partners across Europe If you are interested in distributing our products we offer flexible partnership options and competitive wholesale prices Our goal is to establish long term cooperation with reliable partners and grow together in the expanding E Bike market If you would like more details or have specific requirements reach out to us For an exact price calculation and shipping cost estimation please send us the address and we will get back to you as soon as possible We look forward to your inquiry and the opportunity to work together Best regards Johannes Keller

To prepare for her role as a vindictive chef in Ysabella (2007), Santos took a four-month culinary course at the Center for Asian Culinary Studies. Santos said working on the show helped "hone her skills in these two fields" and proved that she "can also do something aside from acting". She was later cast in Topel Lee's horror thriller Ouija (2007) alongside Jolina Magdangal, Iza Calzado, and Rhian Ramos. The film was met with mixed reviews; Philip Cu-Unjieng of The Philippine Star wrote, "There is nothing earth-shaking in the treatment of the material or the performances he elicits from the ensemble." Jocelyn Dimaculangan, however, noted Santos's "restrained performance". Her final appearance that year was in the comedy drama Sakal, Sakali, Saklolo, the sequel to Kasal, Kasali, Kasalo. The response from critics was overwhelmingly negative; the Philippine Daily Inquirer's Nestor Torre Jr. dismissed the film for its "thrice-told plots and exhausted stellar performances", while Asilo
stated that it "meanders into a list of issues that eventually clutter up its exposition and focus". Despite poor critical reception, the film grossed ?122 million (US$2.3 million) at the box office.

2008–2013: Established actress
Santos co-produced and starred in the independent drama Ploning (2008), which was the Philippine submission for Best Foreign Language Film at the 81st Academy Awards. Jimenez-David wrote that her performance showed "maturity and ripeness, a depth of character that hints at a life lived fully and well", and described the film as "moving, dazzling, and yet intimate and comforting". Santos said she considered taking the part as a "start of a new chapter" in her career. The film was screened at the Palm Springs International Film Festival, Newport Beach Film Festival, and Christopher B. Smith Rafael Film Center in the United States. Santos's next role in 2008 was in Jun Lana's psychological horror thriller Mag-ingat Ka Sa... Kulam as a woman who questions her own sanity after a car accident leaves her with amnesia. Critic Karen Caliwara commended Santos's "versatility" and considered her to be the film's prime asset. In 2009, Santos worked with Agoncillo and Javier Reyes for the third ti
me in the sitcom George and Cecil, which explored a reversal of gender

Outside, cold and blustery weather returned to Adams County after several days of spring like temperatures. Inside, fragrant blossoms and shiny green leaves on our greenhouse window plants and dwarf citrus trees provide an antidote to the whipping winds. Nothing makes a room smell more like spring than the soft lemony scent of the blossoms of a Meyer lemon tree. Citrus from Seed? Dwarf Citrus Tree, Improved Meyer Lemon (Plant and Planter) Citrus lemon 'Improved Meyer' Children often wonder if they can grow a tree from the seeds found inside oranges, tangerines, oranges, lemons, and grapefruit. Yes, as long as the seed is not damaged, the odds are good that it will produce a plant. But, you need to know that seedlings experience a very long period before they flower and bear fruit- perhaps seven years or more! Most citrus grown for indoor home use are dwarf varieties that are either cuttings grown or grafted on a dwarfing rootstock. By using cuttings or grafts from mature trees, a plant is produced that is ready to begin fruiting right away. According to GFEKXGKWCOUEX8KYDHDWTL0200G6YH, garden writer for the E34OJ204425EQSG, if you are serious about citrus, consider trees that are sold for pot culture like the Meyer lemon and other dwarf varieties. The "improved" Meyer Lemon tree is the most popular of all citrus trees, easy to grow, prolific in its blooming and fruiting, and a favorite of chefs for gourmet recipes. If you are looking for an unusual plant to give as a gift, consider a dwarf kumquat, lemon, tangerine, or a handsome calamondin orange with small white blooms that perfume the house. Citrus Varieties That Thrive Indoors Calamondin- The plants have wide, lush leaves and bright orange fruit that resemble small tangerines. Kaffir lime- A lime tree that also boasts delicious leaves used in cooking Southeast Asian dishes. Meiwa kumquat- These kumquats are sweet, round, and perfect for fresh eating. Meyer lemon- The lemons are deep yellow and the trees will bear fruit heavily. Caring for Indoor Citrus Here is a brief guide for keeping your indoor citrus plants happy and healthy: Location: Make sure your plants receive five to six hours of sunlight. Set your tree in a southern exposure, if possible, or supplement the light by using 40 watt fluorescent shop lights above the plants. Fertilizer: Use an acidic fertilizer during the plant's active growing season in late winter, June and August. Use a high-nitrogen-low phosphorus food (20-10-10) or one specifically for citrus every time you water. Water: Your pot must have good drainage as you water every few days. Mist the leaves and give your tree a shower occasionally. Humidity: Homes in winter have drier air, so try a tray of pebbles under the pot. With the right care and a little luck, your own personal citrus grove will bring you great enjoyment and last for years. Just imagine strolling into your living room or kitchen to pluck a lemon for use in your favorite lemon bar recipe or limes for Tex-Mex dressing. subject: PayPal
body: PayPal April 2007 Dear users of PayPal services, Due to upcoming April 2007, and recent changes in PayPal's Service Agreement you need to submit additional details on your PayPal account. Starting from 2007 all PayPal accounts will come with complete detailed information! Identity protection matters. And PayPal works day and night to help keep your identity safe. Identity protection matters. Get Verified! According the new changes in Service Agreement any unverified account will be deleted from the system in 72 hours after receiving this letter. subject: Re: Inquiry about IAM service role inheritance
body:
Dear Isiah,

Thank you for your interest in Aetheros IAM. We appreciate you reaching out.

To answer your question, yes, our IAM service supports role inheritance. You can define parent roles with specific permissions and create child roles that inherit those permissions. Child roles can also have additional, specific permissions that are not granted to the parent role.

For detailed information and examples, please refer to our documentation on role-based access control: [link to documentation]

Let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
Aetheros Support

subject: Dealer Unmasked
body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: There’s Profit In Baby Formula Despite The Dominance Of Industry GiantsADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER • Newsletters • MyForbesGood morning, Friday is here, and we have your headlines: Summer travel planning is in full swing; here are tips on avoiding scams. The identity of a notorious malware dealer was exposed, a first for the PGA Championship and the latest developments in last month's murder of Cash App creator Bob Lee.Hope you have a restful weekend, Sarah Whitmire Senior Editor, Newsletters Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSSOPA IMAGES/LIGHTROCKET VIA GETTY IMAGESDisney scrapped a $1 billion plan that would have created more than 2,000 jobs in Florida, amid an ongoing culture war with Gov. Ron DeSantis, a likely Republican presidential candidate. A DeSantis spokesperson said the decision was “unsurprising” and blamed Disney’s “financial straits, falling market cap and declining stock price.” Tech executive Nima Momeni pleaded not guilty for allegedly stabbing fellow tech executive Bob Lee to death last month in San Francisco. More details have emerged about Lee’s relationship with Momeni’s sister, as well as his lifestyle, reportedly filled with partying and drugs, according to the Wall Street Journal. BUSINESS + FINANCEA key Department of Labor survey could be overestimating the number of job openings by as many as 300,000, according to economists at Goldman Sachs. JOLTS, a monthly poll of employers, has suffered a roughly 50% reduction in its response rate since early 2020. The survey’s standard error is now 90% higher than it was between 2002 and 2013.British telecom giant BT said it will cut 55,000 jobs by 2030, more than 40% of its workforce, and replace about 10,000 jobs with AI tools. The company is currently in a nationwide fiber rollout, and a telecom workers’ union said they had expected the newer infrastructure would lead to lower labor requirements. WEALTH + ENTREPRENEURSHIPJEFF SCIORTINO/THE FORBES COLLECTIONBillionaire Sam Zell died Thursday at 81, according to his company, Equity Residential. The company did not cite a cause of death. Nicknamed the “grave dancer” for his knack at scooping up undervalued properties, Zell built one of the largest real estate empires in the U.S. and was worth $5.2 billion at the time of his passing.TECH + INNOVATIONESENTIREThe identity of “Jack,” a mysterious man who distributes malware (known as Golden Chickens) to some of the most notorious Russian cybercrime gangs, has been revealed and shared with American law enforcement, according to cybersecurity company eSentire. The company declined to share his name with Forbes, as Jack’s life may be in danger: he has a $200,000 bounty out for information on his identity, issued by another hacker who claimed he had been robbed of $1 million.Fintech startup Ramp announced new OpenAI-powered tools for businesses to help automate customer service tasks, as well as a new slate of investors, including Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, chief executives from Quora and Instacart, as well as Stanford AI Lab professor Chris Ré. The company wouldn’t disclose the size of its investments.MONEY + POLITICSConservative activist Ginni Thomas, the wife of Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas, has contributed at least $20,000 to Republican political committees, according to state and federal records. Former President Donald Trump has been one of the biggest recipients of her generosity; she has donated more than $2,500 to political committees backing him, mostly in 2016.SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTThe PGA Championship kicked off yesterday with a unique golfer: 43-year-old Topgolf instructor Gabe Reynolds, the first to ever qualify from a golf entertainment venue. Most of his practice time was at Topgolf rather than a golf course—he’s taught an estimated 1,000 lessons over the past year.Janice Simpson, who owns 10% of the Texas Rangers, put her stake up for sale at a price that places a non-control valuation on the MLB team of $3 billion. The last MLB team that had a control stake change hands was the New York Mets, purchased by Steve Cohen in 2020 for $2.42 billion. Daily Cover Story Bobbie cofounders Laura Modi, left, and Sarah Hardy BobbieStartup Bobbie Proves There’s Profit In Baby Formula Despite The Dominance Of Industry GiantsREAD ARTICLETOPLINEOnly 7% of venture-backed companies that disclose sales ever reach $100 million in annual revenue, but in the wake of a historic shortage, baby formula startup Bobbie hit that mark in just five years. Even more notable, the women-led company achieved this feat in a market already dominated by a handful of multinational brands: Enfamil takes 38%, Gerber Good Start has 10% and Similac commands the lion’s share at 42%. That dominance is part of what sparked a national shortage after Abbott Nutrition voluntarily recalled Similac from shelves in early 2022, leading to panic and concern among the 62% of American parents who use baby formula. Bobbie’s CEO Laura Modi had a perhaps counterintuitive approach to the shortage: facing a doubling of customers who could quickly gobble up her inventory, she chose to close Bobbie’s website to new customers. For six months last year, at the height of the shortage, Bobbie only shipped its product to existing subscribers. “Laura at her core is a community builder,” says Amy Griffin, founder and managing partner of G9 Ventures, a Bobbie investor. “When the product meets the community, you earn brand loyalty.”WHY IT MATTERSModi and her cofounder, COO Sarah Hardy, were both vocal about their breastfeeding issues prior to launching Bobbie in 2018, and lamented the lack of baby formula options in the U.S. Long before they had a product, they reached out to a panel of other new moms to inform research and development, and as word of mouth spread, so did Bobbie’s reach. “If something lights up in the mom’s community, it tends to light up really fast, because there’s a lot of new moms talking about similar things,” says consumer trends and branding expert Libby Rodney.MOREMeet The Entrepreneur Helping Women In Chemo Keep Their HairADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSAs summer travel picks up, so too will scammers and cyberthieves—always be wary of a deal that seems too good to be true—here are findings from McAfee’s new Safer Summer Holidays Travel report: 56%Travelers who said inflation makes them more inclined to look for travel bargains online. 35%Travelers who say they’re more likely to use an unfamiliar booking site to snag a good price. 30%Adults who have fallen victim to a scam, or know someone who has, when booking travel. STRATEGY AND SUCCESSIf the U.S. government does breach the debt ceiling, expect it to hit the housing market like a natural disaster. The markets most affected would likely be places with a lot of federal employees, like Washington, D.C. or Virginia Beach, and as well as areas with the highest shares of older people who might rely on social security payments. subject: A harsh harsh truth
body:
You're receiving this email because you subscribed after being recommended by Techpresso. If you'd like to unsubscribe, you can click here.

Here’s a harsh truth that might change your life…

You must develop the ability to be disliked in order to free yourself from the prison of other people’s opinions.

Remember yesterday I told you about my newsletter you signed up for?

It’s called Your Next Breakthrough and every Monday, I’ll send you harsh, life-changing truths along with a question to ask yourself, and an exercise to try out.

So, here’s today’s question:

What are three things in your life that you are willing to be disliked for?

If you have trouble answering that question, that is a problem.

Tomorrow, I’ll share an exercise you can try out to help you further develop the ability to be disliked. And an inspiring story from one of our readers that may spark an interesting breakthrough for you.

subject: Sticky summer for inflation
body: Plus, China’s demographic decline and Hillary Clinton in conversation ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ All newsletters | Read in browser Roula Khalaf Editor ‌ ‌ May 25 2023 Hello from London, where the newsroom has been reporting on rather bad economic news: UK inflation fell to 8.7 per cent in April, according to the Office for National Statistics — a much smaller drop than the Bank of England expected. UK bond markets jolted at the news, with gilt yields nearing the levels of last year’s “mini” Budget crisis. Inflation in the UK is now about double the equivalent US rate, and well above almost all of western Europe. The return of high inflation and slow growth is reminiscent of the 1970s. The FT’s economics editor Chris Giles is therefore asking: Is the UK once again the ‘sick man’ of Europe?Traders predict more trouble is ahead for UK bonds, after stronger than expected inflation data yesterday © Reuters My choices this week ‌ ‌ US legislators have been drawing up rules that affect their personal investments because of antiquated loopholes in conflict of interest laws. This excellent FT analysis exposes how lawmakers could be benefiting from Capitol Hill’s failure to keep pace with a transformed investment sector.Russia is fortifying its frontline in anticipation of the long-awaited Ukrainian counter-offensive. By analysing satellite imagery and mapping changes on the ground, our correspondent in Kyiv explores how the attack might play out. (free to read)“Forever chemicals” that are vital to the production of microchips could have life-threatening health and environmental effects, growing evidence suggests. Facing calls for more stringent regulation, how is a rapidly growing industry responding to the threat?(If you’d like to read this article for free, please download our iPhone app, FT Edit, where it is in today’s edition. You don’t need to register or give payment details, you can simply download and start reading FT Edit, and the first month is free.)The G7 heads of government painted a picture of unipolar economic dominance at their summit in Hiroshima last weekend. But despite its best efforts, the G7 cannot run the world, writes the FT’s chief economics commentator Martin Wolf.China’s rapidly ageing population — in part a legacy of its one-child policy — is forcing the government to make painful reforms tackling pensions, healthcare and the workforce. This poignant report from Rudong, China’s oldest county, offers a snapshot of China’s demographic decline. (free to read)Last weekend I was in Washington DC for a brilliant day at the FT Weekend festival. Among other highlights, the 2016 Democratic presidential candidate Hillary Clinton discussed China, Putin, Trump and Netflix series The Diplomat, with the FT’s US national editor Ed Luce. subject: Prizes and Awards: It's That Time of Year
body: Dr. Dobb's Update-January 1, 2001 Editor's Note Prizes and Awards: It's That Time of Year 'Tis the season for prizes, or so it seems anyway. For starters, Edmund M. Clarke, E. Allen Emerson, and Joseph Sifakis received the 2007 Turing Award, the granddaddy of all computing awards, for their work on model checking, an automated technique for finding design errors in complex hardware and software. They share a $250,000 prize. Moving from computing to mathematics, Pierre Deligne, Phillip Griffiths, and David Mumford are sharing the 2008 Wolf Foundation Prize in Mathematics. Deligne was honored "for his work on mixed Hodge theory, the Weil conjectures, the Riemann-Hilbert correspondence, and for his contributions to arithmetic," Griffiths "for his work on variations of Hodge structures; the theory of periods of abelian integrals; and for his contributions to complex differential geometry," and Mumford "for his work on algebraic surfaces; on geometric invariant theory; and for laying the foundations of the modern algebraic theory of moduli of curves and theta functions." The three share a $100,000 award. This year's Crafoord Prize combined the fields of abstract mathematics with astrophysics. Maxim Kontsevich and Edward Witten received the 2008 Crafoord Prize in Mathematics from the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences, while Rashid Sunyaev received the Crafoord Prize in Astronomy. Kontsevich and Witten were cited "for their important contributions to mathematics inspired by modern theoretical physics." Half of the $500,000 prize goes to Kontsevich and Witten, the other half to Sunyaev. Back in the field of computing, the 2007 Gordon Bell Prize went to a team of scientists--Kyle Caspersen, James Glosli, John Gunnels, David Richards, Robert Rudd, and Frederick Streitz--from Lawrence Livermore National Lab and IBM for a unique simulation of a phenomenon known as Kelvin-Helmholtz instability on the BlueGene/L. These gents will divvy up a $10,000 prize. And finally, there's the Dr. Dobb's Excellence In Programming Award, given this year to....uh, I can't tell you. It is still a secret. But the recipient will be honored in the upcoming April 2008 issue of Dr. Dobb's Journal and in person at the SD West 2008 Conference on Wednesday, March 5, 2008. For a list of previous recipients, visit Dr. Dobb's Hall of Fame. Congratulations to one and all for well-deserved honors. -- Jonathan Erickson womjcgkea@ddj.com New Features Continuous Integration and Performance Testing Steven extends continuous integration to perform integration, performance, and load testing. David shares insights about the conference sessions and the upcoming release of Python 3.0. subject: Insurers Balk At Higher Costs | Iconoclast highlights | Revelations from Binance Suit
body: Forbes CxO Newsletter Plus: Forbes Future Of Work Highlights ADVERTISEMENT The haze here in New York has lifted from the wildfires in Canada, but we’re all gearing up for another year of record temperatures – exacerbated by developing El Nino conditions.In addition to the costs to property, growth and human health, climate change is making it harder to insure against such risks.Sean Kevelighan, CEO of the Insurance Information Institute, notes average insured natural catastrophe losses have risen nearly 700 percent since the 1980s–most of which is due to people moving into areas that are prone to risks like wildfires. “We’ve been building more and more in harm’s way,” says Kevelighan in an interview that we published last week. He adds that nine of the top 10 record loss years for the industry have happened since the 2000s. That means rising rates or situations in which insurers exit a state altogether. (Insurance is regulated and priced at the state level.) “When you increase the risk, it also increases the price of that risk transfer tool.”State Farm, which insured more than 20% of all homes in California last year, said last month that it would no longer sell new insurance policies for homeowners or business property owners in the state. While wildfires and rising construction costs were cited as major factors in that decision, Kevelighan says insurers are also reacting to regulations that make it difficult to operate–such as California’s “antiquated” proposition 103. The 35-year-old law makes it almost impossible to set new premiums based on computer models that project future risks such as climate change–effectively capping rates. As a result, he says, companies can’t set prices to reflect the real risks. In Florida, meanwhile, the risks are not just related to climate change but also a legal system that has let people hand their insurance claims to a third-party attorney or contractor who can fight in court to get them paid. The result has been a disproportionate amount of claims are fought in court–which recent reforms start to address. Have a great week. Diane Brady Assistant Managing Editor, Communities & Leadership Become a Forbes member today to unlock unlimited access to all of our stories, invitations to members-only events with industry leaders, early access to select new product launches and more. What's Next Highlights from the Forbes Iconoclast Summit: David Solomon, CEO of Goldman Sachs, told chief content officer Randall Lane that Inflation will likely be be a tough challenge for small and regional banks. Here are some other round-ups from the event.Judge Blocks Microsoft-Activision Deal: A California federal court's temporary restraining order pauses a deal that has invited regulatory scrutiny both in the U.S. and abroad.Surprising Revelations From The SEC's Binance Lawsuit: The Securities and Exchange Commission is suing the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange, its U.S. affiliate and founder Changpeng Zhao.Commencement Chronicles: “Every right move I’ve made has come from listening deeply and following that still, small voice," Oprah Winfrey said in a commencement speech at her alma mater, Tennessee State University. Emmy Lucas picks out memorable career advice from some of this year's high-profile speakers. The Mysterious Death Of Forbes Under 30 African Entrepreneur Christian Kazadi: After attending the Forbes Under 30 Africa summit in Botswana, up and coming entrepreneur Christian Kazadi was found dead in police custody in DR Congo. A year later, his family still have no answers. Salesforce And CEO Marc Benioff Make Big AI Push Around Trust: “The most important thing we are doing in AI is trusted and responsible AI,” Benioff told partners, employees, analysts and press earlier this week.Pussy Riot's Nadya Tolokonnikova Reacts To Being Put On Russia's Most Wanted Criminal Suspects List: If you missed it, here's an interview with the brave and creative cofounder of the feminist protest and performance art group. California’s $100 Billion Electric Bullet Train Will Be Fully Solar Powered. : The initial segment of the country’s most expensive infrastructure project is getting a utility-scale clean power system.
